items.title: Gas and oil prices soar and shares tumble on fears conflict could escalate
items.summary: Markets react as the conflict in the Middle East intensifies and concerns grow over how long it will last.

items.title: US and Ecuador forces launch operation to fight drug trafficking
items.summary: Washington says the two countries are combatting what the US calls designated terrorist organisations in the South American country.

items.title: What to know about the 'two sessions',  China's biggest political meeting
items.summary: The carefully choreographed annual meetings signal the priorities of the world's second-largest economy.

items.title: TikTok won't protect DMs with controversial privacy tech, saying it would put users at risk
items.summary: TikTok tells the BBC it won't join rival platforms such as WhatsApp and Messenger in using end-to-end encryption.

items.title: South Korea woman and doctors guilty of murder of newborn baby
items.summary: The case has gripped South Korea, where there is no regulation on how far into pregnancy an abortion can take place.

items.title: US commerce secretary to testify before Congress about Epstein ties
items.summary: The cabinet member "voluntarily" agreed to be interviewed, weeks after acknowledging that he visited Epstein's island.

items.title: Guilty verdict for US father of teenage school shooter
items.summary: Prosecutors say Colin Gray, 55, could have prevented his son from killing four people at a high school in Georgia in 2024.

items.title: Australian girl, 8, killed in snowmobile accident in Japan
items.summary: It is the fourth Australian death at a Japanese ski resort this year.

items.title: South Korea’s stock market in meltdown amid US-Iran war
items.summary: Benchmark index plummets as much as 12.2 percent, eclipsing single-day plunge after 9/11 attacks in 2001.

items.title: The Iran strikes could become a midterm reckoning – for Trump and Israel
items.summary: Conservative dissent over the war is fracturing the GOP and testing the durability of US support for Israel.

items.title: Russia, China raise diplomatic voices against US-Israeli attacks on Iran
items.summary: China's foreign minister tells Israel to end attacks; Russian FM Lavrov says no sign Tehran seeking nuclear bomb.

items.title: US Commerce Secretary Lutnick to testify before Congress about Epstein ties
items.summary: Lutnick's relationship with financier has come under scrutiny after files revealed closer ties than previously known.

items.title: US senators fear ‘boots on the ground’ after Iran war briefing
items.summary: Democratic senators voiced alarm after a classified briefing on the US‑Israeli war with Iran.

items.title: Is the US at war with Iran, and will it put boots on the ground?
items.summary: Some of the main questions asked about the military developments in the Middle East, and what we know so far.

items.title: Nepal election: Is the monarchy still a force, two decades after ouster?
items.summary: Ex-King Gyanendra Shah was removed from power 20 years ago. Will a Nepal in political flux give him another chance?

items.title: Protests in Venezuela against US-Israeli war on Iran
items.summary: Protesters in Venezuela have rallied outside the Iranian Embassy to condemn the US-Israeli war on Iran.

items.title: Iran Live Updates: Israel Begins ‘Broad Wave of Strikes’ on Iran’s Infrastructure
items.summary: The U.S. announced the names of four soldiers who were killed by an Iranian drone on Sunday. The leader of the U.S. Central Command said more than 50,000 troops, 200 fighters, two aircraft carriers and bombers were participating in the fighting.

items.title: Israel Seizes Chance to Crush Hezbollah and Remake Mideast Amid Iran War
items.summary: With the war against Iran underway, and the U.S. military as a powerful ally, the Israeli government is seizing its chance to move against other adversaries.

items.title: U.S. Hunts for Militants as the Nigerian Military Is Accused of Inaction
items.summary: The country has one of the most feared armed forces in Africa, yet within its own borders, it has repeatedly failed to stop abductions and attacks.

items.title: In Myanmar, Junta Leader Eyes the Title of President
items.summary: The military ruler of Myanmar, Senior Gen. Min Aung Hlaing, is expected to seek a civilian post to add a veneer of legitimacy after sham elections.
